# truenas_user_group_membership (Resource)

Manages additive supplementary group membership for a TrueNAS local user. Uses read-modify-write semantics: only the declared groups are managed; groups assigned outside this resource are preserved. Multiple `truenas_user_group_membership` blocks for the same user compose additively.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "truenas_group" "ops" {
  name = "ops"
}

resource "truenas_group" "dev" {
  name = "dev"
}

resource "truenas_user" "alice" {
  username          = "alice"
  full_name         = "Alice Example"
  group             = truenas_group.ops.id
  password_disabled = true
}

resource "truenas_user_group_membership" "alice_groups" {
  user_id   = truenas_user.alice.id
  group_ids = [truenas_group.ops.id, truenas_group.dev.id]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `group_ids` (Set of Number) Set of Unix GIDs to add as supplementary groups for the user.
- `user_id` (Number) Unix UID of the user whose supplementary group membership is managed. Changing this forces a new resource.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource identifier. Equal to the Unix UID of the user as a string.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import truenas_user_group_membership.example 1001:200,201
```
